questiontext.text: A number is divisible by 9 if and only if the sum of its digits is divisible by 9. Which of the following is divisible by 9?

feedback.text: Correct. Sum of digits: \(5+4+3+7+8=27\), and \(27\div9=3\). So 54378 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum: \(1+2+3+4+5=15\), not divisible by 9.
Digit sum: \(9+8+7+6+5=35\), not divisible by 9.
Digit sum: \(2+3+4+5+6=20\), not divisible by 9.

questiontext.text: A sector of a circle with radius 6 cm subtends an angle of 60° at the centre. What is the area of the sector? (Use \(\pi \approx \frac{22}{7}\))

feedback.text: Correct. Area of sector = \(\frac{\theta}{360}\pi r^2 = \frac{60}{360}\times\frac{22}{7}\times36 = \frac{1}{6}\times\frac{792}{7} = \frac{132}{7} \approx 18.86\) cm².
6.28 ≈ 2π corresponds to the arc length (not area) for a unit-radius sector; area = \(\frac{1}{6}\pi(6^2)=6\pi\approx18.86\) cm².
36 cm² would be the area of the full circle divided by \(\pi\); sector area = \(\frac{60}{360}\pi(36)\approx18.86\) cm².
113.1 ≈ \(\pi(6^2)\) is the area of the full circle; the 60° sector is \(1/6\) of that ≈ 18.86 cm².

questiontext.text: Find the missing term: 3, 6, 11, 18, 27, ?

feedback.text: Correct. Differences: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11 (odd numbers increasing by 2). Next difference = 11: \(27+11=38\).
Differences are 3,5,7,9,11; the next term is \(27+11=38\), not 36.
The difference pattern is +3,+5,+7,+9,+11; next term = \(27+11=38\), not 40.
33 would require a difference of 6, but the pattern of differences is odd numbers (3,5,7,9,11); next is 38.

questiontext.text: After a 15% discount, the selling price of an article is ₹1190. What was the marked price?

feedback.text: ₹1368.50 = \(1190\times1.15\), which would be the marked price only if SP = MP/1.15; the correct relation is SP = 0.85 × MP, giving MP = 1400.

questiontext.text: A researcher wants to study the effect of sleep on memory. She divides participants into two groups: one sleeps 8 hours, the other 4 hours, and both take the same memory test. This is an example of

answer.text: An experimental design with a control group (8 hours, normal sleep) and a treatment group (4 hours, sleep deprivation)

feedback.text: Correct. The researcher manipulates the independent variable (sleep duration) and measures the dependent variable (memory test score); one group serves as a comparison baseline (control), making it an experimental design.

answer.text: A correlational study, since sleep and memory are being observed without manipulation

feedback.text: In a correlational study, variables are observed as they naturally occur; here sleep duration is actively manipulated (assigned), making it an experiment, not a correlational study.

answer.text: A case study of individual sleep patterns

feedback.text: A case study examines a single individual or small group in depth; this study has two groups and a comparison, making it an experimental design.

answer.text: A meta-analysis combining results from multiple prior studies

feedback.text: A meta-analysis synthesises data from existing studies; this is a new experiment with directly recruited participants.

questiontext.text: The sum of all terms of an infinite geometric series is 3 and the sum of its first two terms is 8/3. What is the common ratio?

feedback.text: Correct. \(S_\infty = a/(1-r) = 3\) so \(a = 3(1-r)\). First two terms: \(a + ar = a(1+r) = 8/3\). Substituting: \(3(1-r)(1+r) = 8/3 \Rightarrow 3(1-r^2) = 8/3 \Rightarrow 1-r^2 = 8/9 \Rightarrow r^2=1/9 \Rightarrow r=1/3\) (taking positive root for convergence).

questiontext.text: A right circular cone has base radius 7 cm and slant height 25 cm. Its total surface area is (use \(\pi = 22/7\))

feedback.text: Correct. Lateral surface = \(\pi r l = \frac{22}{7}\times7\times25 = 22\times25 = 550\) cm². Base area = \(\pi r^2 = \frac{22}{7}\times49 = 154\) cm². Total = \(550+154=704\) cm².
550 cm² is only the lateral (curved) surface area; the total surface area includes the base circle (154 cm²), giving 704 cm².
\(\pi r(l+r)=\frac{22}{7}\times7\times(25+7)=22\times32=704\) cm², not 770.
154 cm² is only the base area; the total surface area (lateral + base) = 704 cm².

questiontext.text: In Boolean algebra, \(A \cdot (A + B)\) simplifies to

feedback.text: Correct. By the absorption law: \(A\cdot(A+B) = A\cdot A + A\cdot B = A + A\cdot B = A(1+B) = A\cdot1 = A\).
By the absorption law, \(A\cdot(A+B)=A\), not \(A+B\).
The absorption gives \(A\), not \(AB\); distributing: \(A^2+AB=A+AB=A(1+B)=A\).
\(A\cdot(A+B)=A\), which equals 1 only if \(A=1\); in general it equals \(A\).

questiontext.text: A student scores 70 in a test worth 40% of the grade, 80 in a test worth 30%, and 90 in a test worth 30%. What is the weighted average score?

feedback.text: Correct. Weighted average = \(0.4\times70 + 0.3\times80 + 0.3\times90 = 28 + 24 + 27 = 79\).
80 is the simple (unweighted) average of all three scores only if weights were equal; weighted: \(0.4(70)+0.3(80)+0.3(90)=79\).
Weighted average = \(28+24+27=79\neq75\).

questiontext.text: If \(A = \begin{pmatrix}1&2\\3&4\end{pmatrix}\), what is \(\det(A)\)?

feedback.text: Correct. \(\det(A) = 1\times4 - 2\times3 = 4-6 = -2\).
\(1\times4+2\times3=10\) uses addition instead of the determinant formula (which uses subtraction of the cross-product).
\(\det=4-6=-2\); the sign matters — the determinant is \(-2\), not \(+2\).
7 is not the determinant of this matrix; \(\det=1(4)-2(3)=-2\).

questiontext.text: At what rate percent per annum will ₹2000 amount to ₹2205 in 2 years, if interest is compounded annually?

feedback.text: Correct. \(2205 = 2000(1+r)^2 \Rightarrow (1+r)^2 = 2205/2000 = 1.1025 \Rightarrow 1+r = 1.05 \Rightarrow r = 5\%\).

questiontext.text: To determine the area of a triangle, which of the following is sufficient (alone)?

answer.text: All three side lengths (by Heron's formula)

feedback.text: Correct. Given all three sides \(a, b, c\), Heron's formula gives the area: \(A = \sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)}\) where \(s=(a+b+c)/2\).

Two sides alone are insufficient; the area depends on the included angle between them: \(A=\frac{1}{2}ab\sin C\). Without the angle, infinitely many triangles with different areas share the same two sides.
Given only the perimeter, the triangle's shape (and area) is not uniquely determined; e.g., an equilateral and a degenerate triangle can share the same perimeter but have different areas.
Two angles determine the shape (the third angle is fixed) but not the size; the area is not determined without at least one side length.

questiontext.text: The sum of all even numbers from 2 to 50 is

feedback.text: Correct. Even numbers 2,4,6,…,50: there are 25 terms. Sum = \(25\times\frac{2+50}{2} = 25\times26 = 650\).
The sum is \(25\times(2+50)/2 = 25\times26=650\neq600\).
625 = 25² is not the sum of even numbers 2 to 50; that sum is \(25\times26=650\).
700 would require 25 terms with average 28; the average here is \((2+50)/2=26\), giving sum \(25\times26=650\).

questiontext.text: The negation of the statement "Some students passed the exam" is

feedback.text: Correct. The negation of "There exists at least one student who passed" (\(\exists x: P(x)\)) is "For all students, they did not pass" (\(\forall x: \neg P(x)\)), i.e., "No student passed."

answer.text: Some students did not pass the exam

feedback.text: This is not the negation; "some did not pass" is consistent with "some passed." The negation must deny any student passing.
"All passed" is a stronger statement than "some passed" but is not its negation; the negation is "no student passed."

answer.text: Not all students passed the exam

feedback.text: "Not all passed" is equivalent to "some did not pass," which is the negation of "all passed" — not the negation of "some passed."

questiontext.text: A solid sphere has radius 6 cm. Its volume is (use \(\pi = 22/7\))

feedback.text: Correct. \(V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 = \frac{4}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times216 = \frac{4\times22\times216}{21} = \frac{19008}{21} = 904\frac{4}{7}\approx904.76\) cm³. Let me recompute: \(\frac{4}{3}\times\frac{22}{7}\times6^3=\frac{4\times22\times216}{21}=\frac{19008}{21}=905.14\) cm³.
452.57 ≈ \(V/2\); the full sphere volume is \(\frac{4}{3}\pi(6)^3\approx904.76\) cm³.
\(216\pi\) would be the volume of a sphere with radius \((3\times216/4)^{1/3}\); the correct formula gives \(\frac{4}{3}\pi(6)^3=288\pi\approx904.8\) cm³.
\(144\pi\approx452.4\) cm³ is the volume of a hemisphere (half sphere), not the full sphere.

questiontext.text: Three fair coins are tossed simultaneously. What is the probability of getting at least 2 heads?

feedback.text: Correct. Favourable outcomes (at least 2 heads): HHH, HHT, HTH, THH — 4 outcomes. Total: \(2^3=8\). Probability = \(4/8=1/2\).
3/8 is the probability of exactly 2 heads (\(\binom{3}{2}/8=3/8\)); at least 2 heads includes HHH as well, giving \(4/8=1/2\).
1/8 is the probability of exactly 3 heads (HHH only); at least 2 heads includes HHT,HTH,THH too, giving 4/8=1/2.
5/8 is the probability of at least 1 head (\(1-1/8\) minus the 1-head cases... actually P(at least 1)=7/8); P(at least 2 heads)=4/8=1/2.

questiontext.text: The "biodiversity hotspot" concept identifies regions with high concentrations of endemic species and significant habitat loss. How many biodiversity hotspots are currently recognized globally (as of 2023)?

feedback.text: Correct. As of 2023, Conservation International recognizes 36 global biodiversity hotspots. India's Western Ghats and Himalayas are among them.
17 is the number of "megadiverse countries" (a different classification); there are 36 biodiversity hotspots.
25 was the earlier count; the list has been expanded to 36 hotspots as of recent assessments.
There are 36 recognized hotspots globally, not 50.

questiontext.text: Kilogram : Mass :: Ampere : ?

feedback.text: Correct. Kilogram is the SI unit of mass; Ampere (A) is the SI base unit of electric current.
Voltage is measured in Volts (V), not Amperes; Ampere measures electric current.
Resistance is measured in Ohms (Ω); Ampere is the unit of electric current.
Power is measured in Watts (W); the Ampere is the SI unit for electric current.

questiontext.text: A factory produces 600 units per day and sells 540 units per day. After how many days will the surplus reach 1200 units?

feedback.text: Correct. Daily surplus = \(600-540=60\) units. Days to accumulate 1200: \(1200/60=20\) days.
In 10 days, surplus = \(10\times60=600\neq1200\).
In 25 days, surplus = \(25\times60=1500>1200\); the target of 1200 is reached at exactly 20 days.
In 15 days, surplus = \(15\times60=900\neq1200\).

questiontext.text: Which Indian mathematician is credited with independently discovering infinite series expansions for \(\sin\), \(\cos\), and \(\arctan\) (the "Gregory-Leibniz" series for \(\pi\)) several centuries before their European rediscovery?

answer.text: Madhava of Sangamagrama (c. 1340–1425, Kerala School of Mathematics)

feedback.text: Correct. Madhava of Sangamagrama, founder of the Kerala School, derived infinite power series for \(\sin x\), \(\cos x\), and \(\arctan x\) around 1400 CE — predating Newton, Gregory, and Leibniz by about 200 years.
Aryabhata made seminal contributions to trigonometry and algebra, but the infinite series for \(\sin\), \(\cos\), \(\arctan\) are attributed to Madhava of Sangamagrama.
Brahmagupta advanced arithmetic, algebra (cyclic quadrilaterals, zero), and astronomy, but the power series for trigonometric functions are associated with Madhava.
Ramanujan made extraordinary contributions to number theory, continued fractions, and infinite series, but the \(\sin/\cos/\arctan\) series are attributed to Madhava (~1400 CE).
